data_in.description: Ion image dataset with augmented image pairs (from Data Augmentation module)

data_out.description: Two 512-dimensional representation vectors per input pair

domain_knowledge: ResNet18 is a residual convolutional neural network with 18 layers that enables training of deeper architectures by using skip connections to mitigate gradient vanishing.

Shared-weight encoder pairs enforce identical parameter sets across both branches, ensuring that identical augmented images produce identical representations and different augmentations of the same image produce nearby representations in the learned space.
The 512-dimensional output is a learned bottleneck representation that compresses spatial ion image information into a fixed-size feature vector suitable for contrastive loss computation.
Parameter sharing reduces model complexity and training overhead while enforcing the symmetry assumption required for contrastive learning frameworks that compare augmentation pairs.

cmd: import torch; import torchvision.models as models; encoder = models.resnet18(pretrained=False); encoder.fc = torch.nn.Linear(512, 512); encoder.eval(); print('ResNet18 encoder with 512-D output layer instantiated'); x1 = torch.randn(1, 3, 224, 224); x2 = torch.randn(1, 3, 224, 224); with torch.no_grad(): z1 = encoder(x1); z2 = encoder(x2); assert z1.shape == (1, 512) and z2.shape == (1, 512), f'Output shape mismatch: {z1.shape}, {z2.shape}'; print(f'Validation passed: z1.shape={z1.shape}, z2.shape={z2.shape}'); print('Shared-weight ResNet18 encoder module ready for DeepION contrastive learning')

task_description: Implement a shared-weight ResNet18 encoder pair that accepts two augmented ion images and outputs two 512-dimensional representation vectors for the DeepION contrastive learning framework.
task_objective: Implement a shared-weight ResNet18 encoder pair that accepts two augmented ion images and outputs two 512-dimensional representation vectors.
title: Reconstruct the ResNet18-Based Encoder Module Producing 512-D Representation Vectors
uncertainty_notes: This card was generated by the LLM-assisted pipeline and needs scientific expert review.
Each TracedClaim's evidence_span has been substring-checked against its source section; see logs/llm_calls.jsonl and capsules/<task_id>/quality_report.json for groundedness results.
workflow_description: 1. Define a ResNet18 architecture configured to accept ion image inputs and output 512-dimensional feature vectors. 2. Instantiate a parameter-sharing encoder module consisting of a single ResNet18 instance that will process both augmented images sequentially. 3. Configure the encoder to freeze or selectively train its convolutional and pooling layers according to the contrastive learning objective. 4. Implement the forward pass to accept two augmented ion images as input and propagate each through the shared ResNet18 encoder to produce two distinct 512-dimensional representation vectors. 5. Verify output tensor shapes (batch_size × 512) and numerical stability of the representation vectors.
